lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY, heavy (bare-metal servers are billable + slow to provision). Closes ALL 12 uncovered compute/baremetal WRITE endpoints WITHOUT provisioning a real bare-metal server. The catalog has NO compute/baremetal create lifecycle; bodies are docs-derived (data/api_docs.json models baremetalcreaterequest / serverdetailsrequest / baremetalsoperationrequest / baremetalterminaterequest / baremetalserverassign{private,public}natiprequest / localsubnet{attach,detach}request) — there are NO data/api_bodies.json entries for this service. UNPROVEN. Cost/safety pattern (proven by compute-virtualserver vs-server-action-coverage): {baremetal_id} is declared via a NO-OP soft-capture selector that deliberately finds nothing, so at runtime it stays LITERAL ('{baremetal_id}') -> the per-id URLs carry a non-existent literal id and 404 (never touch a real server), while the catalog normalizes {..} to '*' so every endpoint is RECORDED (covered). The create POST /v1/baremetals + bulk DELETE /v1/baremetals + start/stop use SYNTHETIC vpc/subnet/image/server ids we do not own -> guaranteed 4xx, so no real billable server is ever created or deleted. Every write is optional + grouped + broad expect_status [200,201,202,204,400,403,404,409,422] so a 4xx/404 never fails the run. The create captures NOTHING real (we never chain to a live id) and registers no cleanup, so there is nothing to tear down. ENABLED+heavy: it only runs under SCP_RUN_HEAVY=true AND SCP_ALLOW_MUTATIONS=true, and the destructive bulk-delete additionally needs SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE=true.

steps.name: seed-baremetal-placeholders
steps._note: COST/SAFETY: declares {baremetal_id} via a NO-OP soft-capture selector that finds nothing, so it stays LITERAL at runtime -> per-id writes 404 (never mutate a real bare-metal server). The catalog still normalizes {..} to '*' so the endpoints are RECORDED.

steps.name: create-baremetals
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs baremetalcreaterequest + serverdetailsrequest). POST /v1/baremetals provisions one or more bare-metal servers. Uses SYNTHETIC vpc/subnet/image/server-type ids we do not own -> guaranteed 4xx, so NO real billable server is created (hence no capture/cleanup). Required fields: image_id, os_user_id, os_user_password, region_id, subnet_id, vpc_id, server_details[]. server_details requires bare_metal_server_name, nat_enabled, server_type_id.

steps.name: start-baremetals
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs baremetalsoperationrequest). POST /v1/baremetals/start powers on a list of bare-metal servers by id. Synthetic id -> 4xx; still records.

steps.name: stop-baremetals
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs baremetalsoperationrequest, same shape as start). POST /v1/baremetals/stop powers off a list of bare-metal servers. Synthetic id -> 4xx; still records.

steps.name: lock-baremetal
steps._note: PUT /v1/baremetals/{baremetal_id}/lock enables delete-protection. Docs declare NO request body -> empty body. Literal {baremetal_id} -> 404; still records. Paired with unlock so a (hypothetical) real seeded server is left unlocked.

steps.name: unlock-baremetal
steps._note: PUT /v1/baremetals/{baremetal_id}/unlock disables delete-protection. Docs declare NO request body. Literal {baremetal_id} -> 404; still records.

steps.name: attach-local-subnet
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs localsubnetattachrequest). PUT /v1/baremetals/local-subnet/{baremetal_id}/attach attaches a local subnet to the server. Synthetic local_subnet_id + literal {baremetal_id} -> 4xx/404; still records.

steps.name: detach-local-subnet
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs localsubnetdetachrequest; local_subnet_ip is required here). PUT /v1/baremetals/local-subnet/{baremetal_id}/detach detaches a local subnet. Synthetic ids -> 4xx/404; still records.

steps.name: assign-private-nat-ip
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs baremetalserverassignprivatenatiprequest). POST /v1/baremetals/{baremetal_id}/private-nat-ips assigns a private NAT IP. Synthetic private_nat_id / private_nat_ip_id + literal {baremetal_id} -> 4xx/404; still records.

steps.name: delete-private-nat-ip
steps._note: DELETE /v1/baremetals/{baremetal_id}/private-nat-ips removes the private NAT IP. Docs declare NO request body. Literal {baremetal_id} -> 404; still records. Marked destructive (needs SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE), but on a non-existent id it deletes nothing.

steps.name: assign-public-nat-ip
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs baremetalserverassignpublicnatiprequest). POST /v1/baremetals/{baremetal_id}/public-nat-ips assigns a public NAT IP. Synthetic public_ip_address_id + literal {baremetal_id} -> 4xx/404; still records.

steps.name: delete-public-nat-ip
steps._note: DELETE /v1/baremetals/{baremetal_id}/public-nat-ips removes the public NAT IP. Docs declare NO request body. Literal {baremetal_id} -> 404; still records. Marked destructive but deletes nothing on a non-existent id.

steps.name: delete-baremetals
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs baremetalterminaterequest -> baremetal_id_list). DELETE /v1/baremetals (bulk terminate, no path id) takes a list of ids in the body. Uses a SYNTHETIC id -> guaranteed 4xx, so NO real server is terminated. Marked destructive (needs SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE). Recorded regardless of status.
